title: Fragment: "Amor Aeternus".
content:
Wealth and dominion fade into the mass
    Of the great sea of human right and wrong,
    When once from our possession they must pass;
    But love, though misdirected, is among
    The things which are immortal, and surpass
    All that frail stuff which will be - or which was.
meaning:
"Fragment: "Amor Aeternus"." is a quintessential example of Percy Bysshe Shelley's signature style...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
